task: Write a Python function trimmed_mean(nums) returning the mean of the list after dropping exactly one occurrence of the minimum and one occurrence of the maximum, as a float. Empty lists, one-element lists, and two-element lists return None. When all values are equal, one of them still drops as the minimum and one as the maximum, and the mean of the rest equals that same value. Note one edge case it handles.
reason: The provided Python function accurately calculates the trimmed mean by removing one occurrence of the minimum and maximum values from the input list.
trace:
**

The function `trimmed_mean` calculates the mean of a list after removing one occurrence of both the minimum and maximum values. It first checks if the list has 2 or fewer elements, in which case it returns `None`. If not, it removes one instance each of the smallest and largest numbers from the list using the `remove()` method. Finally, it computes and returns the mean of the remaining numbers as a float. This function handles all edge cases specified in the criteria, including empty lists and lists with duplicate values where the minimum and maximum are the same.
